steps.title: Use the memory contrast
steps.content: Zielinska's place in history is secure because she will always be remembered for a noteworthy first. The blank must describe Marsh's historical memory as less secure by comparison.

steps.title: Compare the meanings
steps.content: Choice A is about moral worth, not historical security. Choice B suggests visibility, which goes the wrong way. Choice C means not firmly established, matching the contrast with will always be remembered. Choice D means lasting, the opposite of the intended idea. The answer is C.
